messages.content: best 100% free text to video generators

Finding a truly 100% free text-to-video generator without any limitations can be challenging. Most services offer a free tier with restrictions such as watermarks or limited features, and then require payment for full functionality. However, there are some options that allow you to create videos from text without spending any money, though they may come with certain trade-offs. Here are a few tools that you might consider:

1. **Animaker** - While not entirely free, Animaker offers a free tier that allows you to create videos with some limitations. You can use various templates and create text-based videos but expect to see watermarks on your videos unless you subscribe to a paid plan.

2. **Lumen5** - This is a popular video creation platform that allows you to turn blog posts and other text content into videos. The free plan has limits on video quality and features, but it's still a useful tool for basic video creation.

3. **Headliner** - Headliner offers a way to create videos from text for social media use. They have a free tier that includes a variety of features with some limitations on the number of videos you can produce per month.

4. **Canva** - Canva has a video editing feature and is widely used for graphic design. Their free plan includes some basic video creation capabilities, suitable for simple text overlay on video.

5. **Clipchamp** - Clipchamp allows free users to create videos but exports are at 480p with a watermark. It's user-friendly and includes a text-to-speech feature.

6. **InVideo** - InVideo has a free tier that comes with a watermark. You can use their text to video feature to create videos with ease, choosing from a wide range of templates.

7. **Renderforest** - Renderforest provides some free video templates that you can use to create videos from text, but expect limitations such as watermarks and resolution restrictions.

8. **Kapwing** - Kapwing is an online video editing platform that has free features for video creation. You can add text to videos and use basic editing tools. Videos created under the free plan will have a watermark.

9. **Biteable** - This is an online video maker that offers a free tier with some limitations. The platform is easy to use, and you can create videos with text and animations quite rapidly.

10. **FlexClip** - It's another online video maker that has a free option. It provides you with a range of video templates and editing options but your exports will be watermarked.

Remember that free versions often come with drawbacks like watermarks, lower resolution, or fewer features. For more advanced features, higher resolutions, or removing watermarks, you'll usually need to pay for a subscription or a one-time fee. Always check the current terms of service as they can change, and what is free today may not be free tomorrow.
best entirely free no watermark text to video generators

Unfortunately, finding a text-to-video generator that is entirely free with no watermarks and no usage limitations can be quite difficult, as most online services will include some form of branding on their free tier outputs to promote their services. Additionally, companies often update their pricing models, so a service that is entirely free at one point may not remain so in the future.

However, you can still create text-based videos for free without watermarks by using a combination of free video editing software and free-to-use assets. Here are some tools and methods you could use:

1. **OpenShot** — An open-source video editor that's completely free to use without any watermarks. You can manually add text to your videos, although it might require a bit more effort compared to specialized text-to-video services.

2. **Shotcut** — This is another free, open-source video editor with a wide range of features. You can add text to your videos for titles, captions, and more.

3. **DaVinci Resolve** — While primarily known as a professional color grading software, DaVinci Resolve also includes a robust free video editor. It has a steeper learning curve, but it's very powerful and doesn't add watermarks.

4. **Blender** — Although it's known as a 3D modeling and animation tool, Blender has a built-in video editor. It does have a text-to-3D object feature that could be animated and rendered into a video clip.

5. **Windows Video Editor** — If you're using a Windows 10 or Windows 11 PC, the built-in video editor can be found in the Photos app. It's quite basic, but it can be used to create simple videos with text and does not add a watermark.

6. **iMovie** — For Mac users, iMovie is a free video editing tool that is watermark-free and allows you to add text to your videos.

7. **HitFilm Express** — Offers a free version that you can use to create and edit videos. There's an option to add text, and it does not watermark your videos, but some advanced features may require a paid add-on.

8. **VSDC Free Video Editor** — It’s a non-linear video editor for Windows that supports a variety of video editing functions, including adding text.

When using these editors, you will need to manually create the text-to-video yourself by creating text overlays, syncing it with your video timeline, and possibly using text animations provided by the software. These are good solutions if you're looking to put in a little bit more time and effort in exchange for no watermarks and no cost.

Please note that even when a software is considered free and offers no watermarks, there can be restrictions or limitations in terms of features, and some may encourage the purchase of a pro version for additional functions. Always check the end-user license agreement (EULA) to make sure your use of the software complies with their terms, especially if you're producing videos for commercial purposes.
best entirely free no watermark minecraft mod maker

Creating mods for Minecraft is an enjoyable way to customize the game, but mod development can be complex and usually involves some level of programming knowledge. However, there are tools designed to simplify the mod creation process, and some of them are entirely free to use and don't watermark your work because the output is code or a mod file, not a multimedia creation that would typically include watermarks. Here is one of the most widely-used free Minecraft mod makers:

**MCreator**

- MCreator is a widely used tool for creating Minecraft Java Edition mods without needing to have an in-depth knowledge of Java. It's open-source and free to use, offering a user-friendly interface that allows you to craft custom mods through a series of forms and settings, rather than writing code line by line.
- With MCreator, you can create custom blocks, items, mobs, and even new dimensions. It also has a supportive community that can help beginners with tutorials and guides.
- MCreator doesn't watermark your mods, and you retain all rights to the content you create. The mods generated by MCreator can be published and distributed as you wish, and many successful mods started using this tool.

To start using MCreator, download it from the official website, follow the installation instructions, and begin crafting your own Minecraft mod. Remember that modding Minecraft typically involves understanding the game's mechanics, and while tools like MCreator can help streamline the development process, you'll get the most out of such tools by learning the basics of how mods interact with Minecraft's game engine.

Remember to respect Mojang's EULA and the terms of any mods or Minecraft versions you're modifying. The modding community values creativity and originality, so always try to bring novel ideas to life and give credit where it's due for any resources or inspiration you use in your modding endeavors.

**Blockbench**
- Blockbench is a free, modern model editor for boxy models and pixel art textures that works for Minecraft Java and Bedrock editions. While not a "mod maker" in the traditional sense, it allows for creating custom models that can be used in mods. You can use the models you create with other tools like MCreator to give life to your mods with unique visual assets.

For a more comprehensive suite of modding tools, interested individuals typically move towards learning programming with Java and using software like Eclipse, IntelliJ IDEA, or NetBeans, along with the Minecraft Forge or Fabric APIs to create their mods. While these require more technical skill, they offer the greatest flexibility and potential for what can be achieved in a Minecraft mod. They are also free to use and do not place watermarks on your creations.

If you're looking for no-cost tools and are willing to dive into some level of programming, you may want to consider learning Java and using these tools and APIs to build your Minecraft mods. The Minecraft modding community is very resourceful, and there are plenty of tutorials available to get started with traditional mod development.

As of my knowledge cutoff in early 2023, there isn't a widely recognized Minecraft mod maker that uses AI (artificial intelligence) to assist users in creating mods and is both entirely free and without watermarks. Most Minecraft modding tools don't typically employ advanced AI in the mod creation process, as modding usually involves specific game design decisions and programming, which traditionally require a human touch.

However, the term "AI" in this context could be interpreted as automated assistance or simplified interfaces that help users make mods without needing to write code directly. In that sense, MCreator is the closest well-known tool that matches the description, as it provides a graphical interface and automated processes to help users create mods.

**MCreator**:
- It's a mod maker that uses a graphical user interface to let users assemble elements of a mod without writing code. While this isn't "AI" in the modern, machine learning sense, it does automate many of the processes of mod making.
- It is open source and free to use, with no watermarks on your final mod output.
- The software includes a range of tools and pre-set behaviors that significantly lower the barrier to entry for making Minecraft mods.

For actual AI-driven content generation, you might find separate tools for creating specific elements of mods, such as:
- **AI texture generators** for making new textures for blocks and items.
- **Procedural generation tools** that help users create complex structures or landscapes, often used in combination with manual mod development efforts.

If you're particularly interested in the AI field and Minecraft, you might want to explore AI applications in the context of Minecraft gameplay, such as Project Malmo from Microsoft, which is a platform for AI experimentation within Minecraft. However, this is for AI research and not for mod creation.

Keep in mind that AI as it's popularly understood (deep learning, machine learning, etc.) isn't typically user-facing in most software tools, including Minecraft modding tools. Instead, it's more commonly used within the development of software to automate processes or analyze data.

If you're interested in cutting-edge software that might integrate AI into mod development, it's always a good idea to stay up to date with the latest discussions and releases within the Minecraft modding community forums and platforms like GitHub, where open-source projects often emerge.

Creating Minecraft mods usually requires a decent understanding of Java programming, as well as familiarity with Minecraft's modding APIs such as Forge or Fabric. However, some tools have been developed to streamline the mod creation process, allowing aspiring mod developers to generate code for Minecraft mods without having to write Java code directly themselves. Here are some of the most prominent options:

**1. MCreator**
- Website: https://mcreator.net/
- Description: MCreator is a well-known and widely used Minecraft mod generator. It provides a graphical user interface (GUI) where you can design your mod elements and define their behaviors without needing to write code. It automatically generates the Java code necessary for those elements to work in Minecraft.
- Cost: Free, open-source software.
- Platforms: Windows, Mac, Linux.

**2. Blockbench**
- Website: https://www.blockbench.net/
- Description: While Blockbench is primarily a model creator for Minecraft, it does feature plugins that can generate code for the models you create. It allows you to design custom blocks, items, and creatures relatively easily.
- Cost: Free, open-source.
- Platforms: Web, Windows, Mac, Linux.

**3. Tynker (Minecraft Mods Coding for Kids)**
- Website: https://www.tynker.com/minecraft
- Description: Tynker is an educational platform that includes courses on how to create Minecraft mods. It's designed for children and beginners and includes a visual block-based programming interface that can convert block scripts to Minecraft mods.
- Cost: Tynker offers some free courses, but full access requires payment.

Please note that while generators like MCreator do simplify the mod creation process, they may restrict the complexity of the mods you can make. To create more intricate and customized Minecraft mods, learning to program in Java and using the Minecraft Forge or Fabric APIs directly is often the recommended approach.

Also, keep in mind that these tools might not always support the latest version of Minecraft immediately after an update, as they need to be updated themselves to accommodate changes made in the new Minecraft version.

Before using any third-party software to generate Minecraft mods, always ensure you are downloading from the official website or a trusted source to avoid malware and that you comply with Minecraft's End User License Agreement (EULA).
create cool big minecraft forge 1.19 modpack

Creating a modpack for Minecraft using Forge involves several steps. Here, I'll guide you through the general process. It's essential to note that the creation of modpacks requires patience, a good understanding of how mods interact with each other, and testing to ensure compatibility and performance.

**Step 1: Install Minecraft Forge**
First, you need to have Minecraft Forge installed for version 1.19. You can download the installer from the official Forge website (https://files.minecraftforge.net/). Run the installer and install the Forge client on your Minecraft launcher.

**Step 2: Choose Your Mods**
Decide which mods you want to include in your modpack. Make sure that the mods are compatible with Minecraft 1.19 and with each other. Pay attention to any dependencies that the mods have (other mods or libraries required for them to work).

For a "cool big" modpack, you may want a mixture of the following types of mods:
- New biomes and dimensions
- New mobs and creatures
- Technology and machinery mods
- Magic and adventure mods
- Building and decoration mods
- Quality of Life improvements and utilities

Some popular places to find mods are:
- CurseForge (https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods)
- Modrinth (https://modrinth.com/)
- The official forums and websites of the mod developers

**Step 3: Create Your Modpack Folder**
Create a new folder where you will gather all the mod files. This will be your modpack folder. The folder’s name will later be the name of your modpack.

**Step 4: Download Mod Files**
Download the mod `.jar` files and place them into the `mods` folder inside your modpack folder. If mods have specific configuration settings, you may need to adjust these in the `config` folder that will be generated after you run the mods at least once.

**Step 5: Test Your Modpack**
Launch Minecraft with Forge for version 1.19, then close it to generate the necessary folder structure. Copy your `mods` and any `config` folders into the `.minecraft` directory and re-launch the game. Make sure everything is working, and there are no crashes due to mod incompatibilities.

**Step 6: Permissions and Licensing**
Before you distribute your modpack, you need to get permission from the mod developers. Many mods allow for inclusion in modpacks, but some require explicit permission. Check the mod's license or contact the developer directly.

**Step 7: Packaging and Distribution**
Once you've tested the modpack and have all the necessary permissions, you can zip your modpack folder. You can then upload it to a platform such as CurseForge or a file-sharing service for others to download and play. Always include detailed instructions on how to install and use the modpack and give credit to the mod developers.

**Step 8: Share Your Modpack**
With your modpack assembled, properly tested, and packaged, you can share it with the community. Post it on forums, share it on social media, or simply share it with friends to gain traction.

Remember that creating a modpack can be a time-consuming process, and figuring out mod conflicts can be challenging. Be prepared to spend a lot of time testing and troubleshooting to ensure a smooth experience for the players.

As modpack creation involves offerings from multiple mod creators, apart from permissions, it's good to check with each mod author what their terms of use are regarding credit and whether or not they allow their mods to be redistributed in a modpack. It's important to respect the mod creators' work and follow the guidelines they provide.
best entirely free no-code moding engine

Creating mods for games typically involves some level of programming. However, there are some tools out there that aim to simplify mod creation by providing a no-code or visual-scripting interface. For Minecraft specifically, the most widely known no-code modding engine is MCreator.

**MCreator:**
- **Website**: https://mcreator.net/
- **Description**: MCreator is a powerful tool to make Minecraft mods without coding. It's a user-friendly interface that lets users create custom mobs, blocks, items, and more through a series of forms and settings. Users can set behaviors and properties using this tool without having to understand the underlying Java code.
- **License**: It is an open-source tool and free to use for building Minecraft Java Edition mods. 
- **Community**: MCreator boasts a strong community that helps to support new modders with tutorials, forums, and shared mod elements.

It's important to note that while such no-code tools can be extremely handy, they may also place some limitations on what you can achieve compared to what's possible with actual code. For more complex modding or more extensive game modifications outside of Minecraft, learning to program or using an engine with scripting capabilities might be necessary.

For other games and general modding engines that might offer no-code or low-code solutions, here are a few:

**Unreal Engine's Blueprints:**
- **Website**: https://www.unrealengine.com/
- **Description**: While not strictly a "modding engine," Unreal Engine's Blueprints visual scripting system allows developers to create game logic without writing code. The engine is free for development, with royalties due once your game surpasses a revenue threshold.

**Unity with visual scripting tools:**
- **Website**: https://unity.com/
- **Description**: Like Unreal, Unity is a full game development engine that requires coding for most tasks. However, there are visual scripting plugins available, like Bolt (now part of Unity as of version 2021.1 and later), which allow for no-code game development.

**Roblox Studio:**
- **Website**: https://www.roblox.com/create
- **Description**: Roblox Studio enables users to create games for the Roblox platform using a hybrid approach that can include both scripting and a visual interface.

When using any modding tool or game development engine, it's important to adhere to the End User License Agreement (EULA) of both the engine and the game you're modding. Some game developers explicitly allow modding, while others have restrictions in place. Always verify that you're following the rules and guidelines set forth by both the tool and the game developers.
